Initiation of Visit: This visit was initiated by patient_. The chief complaint is needing
MEDICAL NECESSITY: This visit with a FTF encounter with the patient in the home is not
a visit of convenience. Patient is seen in the home because there is a medical necessity for
this visit to be conducted in the patient's environment. It is medically necessary and safer
for the patient to be seen in the patient's own environment today.
Follow-up for pneumonia, decreased appetite and weight loss, lower back pain from hitting
The patient returns for follow-up of pneumonia, reporting significant improvement in
symptoms. His nasal congestion has cleared up quite a bit since taking his prescribed
medication, and he is no longer coughing much. However, he has experienced decreased
appetite and weight loss since completing his antibiotic course one week ago. He describes
eating very little throughout the day - typically just a bowl of cereal with minimal milk and
coffee in the morning, minimal snacking during the day, and small portions at dinner. He
has been nodding off frequently and appears to have lost noticeable weight, though the
The patient also reports a new complaint of lower back tenderness after hitting something
in his bedroom. The area has some swelling and feels tender to him, though it does not
cause significant pain when pressed. He can feel pressure when the area is touched but
Additionally, he experienced diarrhea while on his medication, but this has since resolved
and his bowel movements have returned to normal.
- Recently completed course. Caused diarrhea which has resolved.
General: Positive for decreased appetite and weight loss.
Musculoskeletal: Positive for lower back tenderness.

The patient's speech was normal, sharing conversation with normal laryngeal efforts.
Appropriate mood and affect were seen on exam. Thought processes were logical,
relevant, and thoughts were completed normally. Thought content was normal. Thought
content was normal with no psychotic or suicidal thoughts. The patient's judgement was
realistic with normal insight into their present condition. Mental status included: correct
time, place, person orientation, normal recent and remote memory, normal attention span
and concentration ability. Language skills included the ability to correctly name objects.
Fund of knowledge included normal awareness of current and past events.
General: Patient appears to have lost weight.
Musculoskeletal: Small lump with mild swelling noted in lower back area where patient
reports hitting something. No pain elicited on palpation of the area.

- X-ray: Improved compared to previous study, no fluid present, pneumonia cleared

Patient with resolved pneumonia presenting for follow-up with improved respiratory
symptoms but experiencing decreased appetite and weight loss following recent antibiotic
Assessment: X-ray shows improvement with resolution of pneumonia. Patient reports
significant improvement in nasal congestion and minimal coughing. No fluid present on
imaging. Respiratory symptoms have largely resolved.
- Pneumonia has cleared, no further respiratory treatment needed
Assessment: Patient experiencing decreased appetite and noticeable weight loss following
completion of antibiotic treatment one week ago. Currently eating minimal amounts -
bowl of cereal with little milk for breakfast, coffee, and small dinner portions with minimal
snacking throughout the day. Patient appears to have lost weight on examination.
Symptoms likely related to recent antibiotic course and should improve as medication
- Add Ensure chocolate protein shakes between meals
- Prescribe appetite stimulant (small pill, temporary use)
- Encourage gradual increase in Ensure consumption until finishing full servings
Assessment: Patient reports tenderness in lower back after hitting something in bedroom.
Physical examination reveals small lump with mild swelling, consistent with contusion from
blunt trauma. No significant pain on palpation.
- Monitor for resolution of swelling and tenderness
Advance Care Planning (ACP): A voluntary, face-to-face discussion was held with the
patient regarding goals of medical treatment, advance directives, and preferences for care.
Topics included: treatment goals, values and preferences, resuscitation status,
hospitalization and life-sustaining treatment options, and the role of advance directives
(living will, healthcare proxy, power of attorney). Patient (and family, if applicable) were

CCM- Comprehensive personalized chronic care plan created, revised, and updated, as
needed. Provided a comprehensive care plan to set and track progress towards health
goals, and support between regular face-to-face visits. Spent 30min with patient.
- Continue Home Health Services to prevent injuries to self, progression of diseases, and
SN to monitor, assess, and for progression of disease.
SN to evaluate knowledge deficits of Pt and family regarding disease processes,
medications, medication regimen, comorbidities, and care plan and educate appropriately.
SN to take VS and log into book for data and history. Report for BP >160 and >90, HR
SN needed d/t high potential for disease progression and inpatient hospitalizations.
SN to evaluate patient's knowledge deficit and teach accordingly the disease process and
management of unstable medical conditions including comorbid factors.
Report s/s of infection or abnormal wound conditions.
Pt needs assistance with medication management, observation and assessment for
Pt will need skilled nursing due to high potential for hospital admission.
Pt will need skilled nursing for teachings of disease process, diet and new medication
PT/OT to evaluate and treat appropriately to increase functionality, decreases debility and
morbidity. Increase strength, balance, and cardio/pulmonary function. Improve ability to
perform ADL's safely and independently. Patient is to improve and/or return to baseline.
- Start taking appetite stimulant medication as prescribed to help improve appetite
- Drink Ensure protein shakes, preferably chocolate flavor
- You don't have to finish the whole Ensure at once - drink it little by little throughout the day
- Try to eat more during the day to prevent further weight loss
- Monitor appetite and weight over the next week
- Contact office if appetite does not improve for possible additional medication
